sourceSectionFilterAuditRows.sectionTitle: The BS in Biomedical Sciences immerses students in an exploration of topics in the life sciences, including cell biology, genetics, microbiology, molecular biology, neurobiology and physiology, as they are applied to the science of human health. The BS in Biomedical Sciences allows students to fulfill some undergraduate course requirements for admission to medical, dental, veterinary, pharmacy and other health- and science-related graduate or professional schools.

requirementCueLines: The BS in Biomedical Sciences immerses students in an exploration of topics in the life sciences, including cell biology, genetics, microbiology, molecular biology, neurobiology and physiology, as they are applied to the science of human health. The BS in Biomedical Sciences allows students to fulfill some undergraduate course requirements for admission to medical, dental, veterinary, pharmacy and other health- and science-related graduate or professional schools.
TPHYS 121 Physics - Mechanics OR PHYS 114 AND PHYS 117 (5)
Complete a minimum of 15 credits of advanced Biomedical Sciences. Select a minimum of two classes from List A, and an additional class from List A or List B.
TCHEM 439 Analytical Chemistry with Environmental Applications
TESC 210 Introductory Research Experience in the Sciences
TESC 433 Pollutant Fate and Transport in the Environment
TBIOMD 310 Foundations in Biomedical Sciences (5 credits, to be completed early in the third year)
TBIOMD 410 Biomedical Sciences Senior Seminar (3 credits, to be completed in the fourth year)
TBIOMD 490 Context for Global Health Experiential Learning AND TBIOMD 491 Global Health Experiential Learning Program
TBIOMD 492 Critical Reading in Biomedical Sciences Literature
TBIOMD 494 Biomedical Sciences Community Engagement Internship
TBIOMD 499 Undergraduate Research in the Biomedical Sciences
TGH 494 Thesis of Project for Global Honors * (with approval from major coordinator)
TGH 496 Experiential Learning in Global Honors * (with approval from major coordinator)
TEGL 210 Introduction to Qualitative Methodology and Research Ethics
THLTH 325 Medical and Ethical Issues in Literature and Culture
TPHIL 455 Medicine and Morality: Issues in Biomedical Ethics
TBIOL 252 The Nature of Human Diversity
TEGL 202 Introduction to Contemporary American Indian Issues
TEGL 464 Indigenous Health, Political Ethnoecology and Governance
TPSYCH 362 Psychophysiology of Stress and Stress Management
chooseStatements: Complete a minimum of 15 credits of advanced Biomedical Sciences. Select a minimum of two classes from List A, and an additional class from List A or List B.

parsedDegreeMapBlockCandidates.title: Biomedical Sciences (BS) parsed official source requirements
sourceLineHints: The BS in Biomedical Sciences immerses students in an exploration of topics in the life sciences, including cell biology, genetics, microbiology, molecular biology, neurobiology and physiology, as they are applied to the science of human health. The BS in Biomedical Sciences allows students to fulfill some undergraduate course requirements for admission to medical, dental, veterinary, pharmacy and other health- and science-related graduate or professional schools.
